volumeTitle: Children of Heracles. Hippolytus. Andromache. Hecuba
label: Hecuba (Section 400 - Section 553)

sections.label: Section 400
sections.original: ὡς τῆσδʼ ἑκοῦσα παιδὸς οὐ μεθήσομαι .
sections.translation: Be sure I will never willingly relinquish my child .

sections.label: Section 401
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐδʼ ἐγὼ μὴν τήνδʼ ἄπειμʼ αὐτοῦ λιπών .
sections.translation: Well , be equally sure I will never go away and leave her here .

sections.label: Section 402
sections.original: μῆτερ , πιθοῦ μοι · καὶ σύ , παῖ Λαερτίου ,
sections.translation: Mother , listen to me ; and you , son of Laertes , make allowance for a parent ’ s natural wrath . My poor mother , do not fight with our masters .

sections.label: Section 405
sections.original: βούλῃ πεσεῖν πρὸς οὖδας ἑλκῶσαί τε σὸν
sections.translation: Will you be thrown to the ground , be roughly thrust aside and wound your aged skin , and in unseemly fashion be torn from me by youthful arms ? This you will suffer ; but do not , for it is not right for you . No , my dear mother ! give me your beloved hand ,

sections.label: Section 410
sections.original: δὸς καὶ παρειὰν προσβαλεῖν παρηίδι ·
sections.translation: and let me press your cheek to mine ; for never again , but now for the last time , shall I behold the dazzling sun-god ’ s orb . Take my last farewells now . O mother , my mother ! I pass beneath the earth .

sections.label: Section 415
sections.original: ὦ θύγατερ , ἡμεῖς δʼ ἐν φάει δουλεύσομεν .
sections.translation: O my daughter , I am still to live and be a slave .

sections.label: Section 416
sections.original: ἄνυμφος ἀνυμέναιος ὧν μʼ ἐχρῆν τυχεῖν .
sections.translation: Unwedded I depart , never having tasted the married joys that were my due !

sections.label: Section 417
sections.original: οἰκτρὰ σύ , τέκνον , ἀθλία δʼ ἐγὼ γυνή .
sections.translation: Yours , my daughter , is a piteous lot , and sad is mine also .

sections.label: Section 418
sections.original: ἐκεῖ δʼ ἐν Ἅιδου κείσομαι χωρὶς σέθεν .
sections.translation: There in Hades ’ courts shall I lie apart from you .

sections.label: Section 419
sections.original: οἴμοι · τί δράσω ; ποῖ τελευτήσω βίον ;
sections.translation: Ah me , what shall I do ? where shall I end my life ?

sections.label: Section 420
sections.original: δούλη θανοῦμαι , πατρὸς οὖσʼ ἐλευθέρου .
sections.translation: Daughter of a free-born father , a slave I am to die .

sections.label: Section 421
sections.original: ἡμεῖς δὲ πεντήκοντά γʼ ἄμμοροι τέκνων .
sections.translation: Not one of all my fifty children left !

sections.label: Section 422
sections.original: τί σοι πρὸς Ἕκτορʼ ἢ γέροντʼ εἴπω πόσιν ;
sections.translation: What message can I take for you to Hector or your aged lord ?

sections.label: Section 423
sections.original: ἄγγελλε πασῶν ἀθλιωτάτην ἐμέ .
sections.translation: Tell them that of all women I am the most miserable .

sections.label: Section 424
sections.original: ὦ στέρνα μαστοί θʼ , οἵ μʼ ἐθρέψαθʼ ἡδέως .
sections.translation: Ah ! breasts and paps that fed me with sweet food !

sections.label: Section 425
sections.original: ὦ τῆς ἀώρου θύγατερ ἀθλίας τύχης .
sections.translation: Oh , my daughter — your wretched , untimely fate !

sections.label: Section 426
sections.original: χαῖρʼ , ὦ τεκοῦσα , χαῖρε Κασάνδρα τʼ ἐμοί ,
sections.translation: Farewell , my mother ! farewell , Cassandra !

sections.label: Section 427
sections.original: χαίρουσιν ἄλλοι , μητρὶ δʼ οὐκ ἔστιν τόδε .
sections.translation: Fare well ! others do , but not your mother , no !

sections.label: Section 428
sections.original: ὅ τʼ ἐν φιλίπποις Θρῃξὶ Πολύδωρος κάσις .
sections.translation: You too , my brother Polydorus , in Thrace , the home of steeds !

sections.label: Section 429
sections.original: εἰ ζῇ γʼ · ἀπιστῶ δʼ · ὧδε πάντα δυστυχῶ .
sections.translation: Yes , if he lives , which I doubt ; so luckless am I in every way .

sections.label: Section 430
sections.original: ζῇ καὶ θανούσης ὄμμα συγκλῄσει τὸ σόν .
sections.translation: He lives ; and , when you die , he will close your eyes .

sections.label: Section 431
sections.original: τέθνηκʼ ἔγωγε πρὶν θανεῖν κακῶν ὕπο .
sections.translation: I am dead ; sorrow has forestalled death here .

sections.label: Section 432
sections.original: κόμιζʼ , Ὀδυσσεῦ , μʼ ἀμφιθεὶς κάρα πέπλοις
sections.translation: Come veil my head , Odysseus , and take me away ; for now , before the fatal blow , my heart is melted by my mother ’ s wailing , and hers by mine .

sections.label: Section 435
sections.original: ὦ φῶς · προσειπεῖν γὰρ σὸν ὄνομʼ ἔξεστί μοι ,
sections.translation: O light of day ! for still I may call you by your name , though now my share in you is only the time I take to go between Achilles ’ tomb and the sword . Odysseus and his attendants lead Polyxena away .

sections.label: Section 438
sections.original: οἲ ʼγώ , προλείπω · λύεται δέ μου μέλη .
sections.translation: Alas ! I faint ; my limbs sink under me . O my daughter , embrace your mother , stretch out your hand ,

sections.label: Section 440
sections.original: δός · μὴ λίπῃς μʼ ἄπαιδʼ . ἀπωλόμην , φίλαι ⋯
sections.translation: give it to me ; do not leave me childless ! Ah , friends ! it is my death-blow . Oh ! to see that Spartan woman , Helen , sister of the sons of Zeus , in such a plight ; for her bright eyes have caused the shameful fall of Troy ’ s once prosperous town . Hecuba sinks fainting to the ground .

sections.label: Section 1.444
sections.original: αὔρα , ποντιὰς αὔρα ,
sections.translation: O breeze , breeze of the sea ,

sections.label: Section 1.445
sections.original: ἅτε ποντοπόρους κομί-
sections.translation: that wafts swift galleys , ocean ’ s coursers , across the surging main ! Where will you bear me , the sorrowful one ? To whose house shall I be brought , to be his slave and chattel ?

sections.label: Section 1.450
sections.original: Δωρίδος ὅρμον αἴας ;
sections.translation: to some haven in the Dorian land , or in Phthia , where men say Apidanus , father of fairest streams , makes fat and rich the soil ?

sections.label: Section 1.455
sections.original: ἢ νάσων , ἁλιήρει
sections.translation: Or to an island home , sent on a voyage of misery by oars that sweep the brine , leading a wretched existence in halls where the first-created palm and the bay-tree put forth their sacred

sections.label: Section 1.460
sections.original: σχε πτόρθους Λατοῖ φίλᾳ ὠ-
sections.translation: shoots for dear Latona , a memorial of her divine birth-pains ? and there with the maids of Delos shall I hymn

sections.label: Section 1.465
sections.original: χρυσέαν ἄμπυκα τόξα τʼ εὐλογήσω ;
sections.translation: the golden head-band and bow of Artemis , their goddess ?

sections.label: Section 2.466
sections.original: ἢ Παλλάδος ἐν πόλει
sections.translation: Or in the city of Pallas , the home of Athena of the lovely chariot , shall I then upon her saffron robe yoke horses ,

sections.label: Section 2.470
sections.original: δαιδαλέαισι ποικίλλουσʼ
sections.translation: embroidering them on my web in brilliant varied shades , or the race of Titans , put to sleep by Zeus the son of Cronos with bolt of flashing flame ?

sections.label: Section 2.475
sections.original: ὤ μοι τεκέων ἐμῶν ,
sections.translation: Alas for my children ! alas for my ancestors , and my country which is falling in smouldering ruin among the smoke , sacked by the Argive spear , while I upon a foreign

sections.label: Section 2.480
sections.original: νᾳ χθονὶ δὴ κέκλημαι δού-
sections.translation: shore am called a slave , indeed ! leaving Asia , Europe ’ s handmaid , and receiving in its place a deadly marriage-bower .

sections.label: Section 484
sections.original: ποῦ τὴν ἄνασσαν δή ποτʼ οὖσαν Ἰλίου
sections.translation: Where can I find Hecuba , who once was

sections.label: Section 485
sections.original: Ἑκάβην ἂν ἐξεύροιμι , Τρῳάδες κόραι ;
sections.translation: queen of Ilium , you Trojan maidens ?

sections.label: Section 486
sections.original: αὕτη πέλας σου νῶτʼ ἔχουσʼ ἐπὶ χθονί ,
sections.translation: There she lies near you , Talthybius , stretched full length upon the ground , wrapped in her robe .

sections.label: Section 488
sections.original: ὦ Ζεῦ , τί λέξω ; πότερά σʼ ἀνθρώπους ὁρᾶν ;
sections.translation: O Zeus ! what can I say ? that your eye is over man ? or that we hold this opinion all to no purpose ,

sections.label: Section 490
sections.original: ψευδῆ , δοκοῦντας δαιμόνων εἶναι γένος
sections.translation: falsely thinking there is any race of gods , when it is chance that rules the mortal sphere ? Was not this the queen of wealthy Phrygia , the wife of Priam highly blessed ? And now her city is utterly overthrown by the foe ,

sections.label: Section 495
sections.original: αὐτὴ δὲ δούλη γραῦς ἄπαις ἐπὶ χθονὶ
sections.translation: and she , a slave in her old age , her children dead , lies upon the ground , soiling her wretched head in the dust . Ah ! old as I am , may death be my lot before I am caught in any shameful mischance . Arise , poor lady ! lift up

sections.label: Section 500
sections.original: πλευρὰν ἔπαιρε καὶ τὸ πάλλευκον κάρα .
sections.translation: yourself and raise that white head .

sections.label: Section 501
sections.original: ἔα · τίς οὗτος σῶμα τοὐμὸν οὐκ ἐᾷ
sections.translation: Oh ! who are you that will not let my body rest ? Why disturb me in my anguish , whoever you are ?

sections.label: Section 503
sections.original: Ταλθύβιος ἥκω Δαναϊδῶν ὑπηρέτης ,
sections.translation: I , Talthybius , have come , the servant of the Danaids ; Agamemnon has sent me for you , lady .

sections.label: Section 505
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατʼ , ἆρα κἄμʼ ἐπισφάξαι τάφῳ
sections.translation: Good friend , have you come because the Achaeans are resolved after all to slay me too at the grave ? How welcome your tidings would be ! Let us hasten and lose no time ; please lead the way , old man .

sections.label: Section 508
sections.original: σὴν παῖδα κατθανοῦσαν ὡς θάψῃς , γύναι ,
sections.translation: I have come to fetch you to bury your daughter ’ s corpse , lady ; and those that send me are

sections.label: Section 510
sections.original: δισσοί τʼ Ἀτρεῖδαι καὶ λεὼς Ἀχαιικός .
sections.translation: the two sons of Atreus and the Achaean people .

sections.label: Section 511
sections.original: οἴμοι , τί λέξεις ; οὐκ ἄρʼ ὡς θανουμένους
sections.translation: Alas ! What will you say ? Have you not after all come to fetch me to my doom , but to announce ill news ? Lost , my child ! snatched from your mother ’ s arms ! and I am childless now , at least as regards you ; ah , woe is me !

sections.label: Section 515
sections.original: πῶς καί νιν ἐξεπράξατʼ ; ἆρʼ αἰδούμενοι ;
sections.translation: How did you end her life ? was any mercy shown ? or did you deal ruthlessly with her as though your victim were a foe , old man ? Speak , though your words must be pain to me .

sections.label: Section 518
sections.original: διπλᾶ με χρῄζεις δάκρυα κερδᾶναι , γύναι ,
sections.translation: Lady , you wish me to have a double benefit of tears in pity for your child ; for now too as I tell the sad tale

sections.label: Section 520
sections.original: τέγξω τόδʼ ὄμμα , πρὸς τάφῳ θʼ ὅτʼ ὤλλυτο .
sections.translation: my eyes will be wet , as they were at the tomb when she was dying .

sections.label: Section 521
sections.original: παρῆν μὲν ὄχλος πᾶς Ἀχαιικοῦ στρατοῦ
sections.translation: All Achaea ’ s army was gathered there in full array before the tomb to see your daughter sacrificed ; and the son of Achilles took Polyxena by the hand and set her on the top of the mound , while I was near ;

sections.label: Section 525
sections.original: λεκτοί τʼ Ἀχαιῶν ἔκκριτοι νεανίαι ,
sections.translation: and a chosen band of young Achaeans followed to hold your child and prevent her struggling . Then Achilles ’ son took in his hands a brimming cup of gold and raised in his hand an offering to his dead father , making a sign to me

sections.label: Section 530
sections.original: σιγὴν Ἀχαιῶν παντὶ κηρῦξαι στρατῷ .
sections.translation: to proclaim silence throughout the Achaean army . So I stood at his side and in their midst proclaimed , Silence , you Achaeans ! let all the people be silent ! peace ! be still ! So I hushed the army . Then he spoke : Son of Peleus , my father ,

sections.label: Section 535
sections.original: δέξαι χοάς μοι τάσδε κηλητηρίους ,
sections.translation: accept the offering I pour for you to appease your spirit , strong to raise the dead ; and come to drink the black blood of a pure girl , which I and the army are offering you ; oh ! be propitious to us ; grant that we may loose our prows and the

sections.label: Section 540
sections.original: νεῶν δὸς ἡμῖν πρευμενοῦς τʼ ἀπʼ Ἰλίου
sections.translation: cables of our ships , and , meeting with a prosperous voyage from Ilium , all come to our country . So he spoke ; and all the army echoed his prayer . Then seizing his golden sword by the hilt he drew it from its scabbard , signing to the picked young Argive

sections.label: Section 545
sections.original: νεανίαις ἔνευσε παρθένον λαβεῖν .
sections.translation: warriors to hold the maid . But she , when she perceived it , uttered this speech : O Argives , who have sacked my city ! of my free will I die ; let no one lay hand on me ; for bravely will I yield my neck .

sections.label: Section 550
sections.original: ἐλευθέραν δέ μʼ , ὡς ἐλευθέρα θάνω ,
sections.translation: By the gods , leave me free ; so slay me , that death may find me free ; for to be called a slave among the dead fills my royal heart with shame .

sections.label: Section 553
sections.original: λαοὶ δʼ ἐπερρόθησαν , Ἀγαμέμνων τʼ ἄναξ
sections.translation: Then the people shouted their applause , and king Agamemnon told the young men to let go the maid .
